    title: "Baryonic Feedback in Cosmological Simulations: State of the Art"

## Summary
Baryonic feedback from active galactic nuclei (AGN) and stellar processes significantly suppresses the matter power spectrum on scales k > 0.5 h/Mpc, biasing cosmological parameter inference from weak lensing and galaxy clustering if unaccounted for. Current simulations disagree on the magnitude of suppression by factors of 2–3.

## Evidence
Hydrodynamical simulations (BAHAMAS, IllustrisTNG, FLAMINGO) consistently show AGN feedback reduces power spectrum amplitude by 5–20% at k ~ 1 h/Mpc, but the exact suppression depends strongly on subgrid AGN feedback prescriptions. X-ray observations of gas fractions in groups and clusters provide calibration constraints, favoring moderate feedback models.

## Significance
Baryonic feedback is a leading systematic uncertainty for Stage-IV surveys (LSST, Euclid, Roman). Failure to correctly model or marginalize over feedback effects could bias dark energy constraints by several sigma, potentially mimicking or masking new physics signals.
